obs: Recently watched 'Hamilton' on Disney+ about three weeks ago and loved it. Seeking similar musicals with music style and themes like Hamilton.
Already watched 'Newsies' and 'Aladdin' on Disney+. Loved the stage design and costumes in Newsies.

Assistant recommended: 'In the Heights' (Lin-Manuel Miranda), 'Freestyle Love Supreme' (improvisational hip-hop by Miranda, Thomas Kail, Anthony Veneziale), 'Passing Strange' (Stew, Heidi Rodewald), 'Bloody, Bloody Andrew Jackson' (Alex Timbers, Michael Friedman).

Interested in learning musical theater history. Podcasts recommended: The Ensemblist, The Fabulous Invalid, Maxamoo, The Broadway Cast. Books: 'Broadway: The American Musical' (Kantor, Maslon), 'Not Since Carrie' (Mandelbaum), 'The Oxford Companion to the American Musical' (Hischak).
Just started actively participating in the 'Book Lovers' group discussions today. Seeking fiction recommendations across multiple genres.

Interested in fantasy and science fiction with strong female protagonists and diverse representation. Liked: 'The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo' (Taylor Jenkins Reid), 'The Night Circus' (Erin Morgenstern), 'Gone Girl' (Gillian Flynn).

Interested in unique sneaker designs blending multiple regional art styles. Examples given: Adidas Ultra Boost 'A Tribe Called Quest', Nike Kyrie 6 Asia Collection, Reebok Classic Nylon 'Himalayan Pack', Vans 'Decades Pack', Puma x Paul Stanley Suede 50 'Gold'.

Planning to sell Toyota Camry soon and tracking maintenance history for potential buyers. Maintenance log entries: 02/10/2022 30k miles oil change $25; 05/15/2022 35k miles tire rotation $20; 08/20/2022 40k miles brake pad replacement $150.

January 2023: 23,456 miles at Toyota dealership - air filter, oil change, tire rotation $250. 01/18/2023: 23,500 miles gas fill-up 15 gallons $35.50 at Shell. 02/12/2023: tire balancing and alignment $80. Car cover purchased from Amazon with free shipping $70.

Feeling disconnected from social circle and spending more time alone than comfortable. Two weeks ago had tough work day, ate takeout alone at home (third time that week). Planning to reconnect with friends through various strategies.

Stayed at Hyatt Regency Chicago about 3 weeks ago for work trip. Hotel conveniently located near conference venue with over 2,000 rooms. Connected to Illinois Center via indoor walkway. Caught up with US colleagues at conference.

Visited Willis Tower Skydeck on 103rd floor and stepped out onto glass Ledge (4.3 feet extension). Also visited Cloud Gate sculpture ('The Bean'). Tried deep-dish pizza at Lou Malnati's restaurant, loved thick crust and flavorful fillings.

Drove to Asheville last month from home, approximately 4.5-hour drive away. Familiar with area and Blue Ridge Parkway scenic route. Interested in exploring attractions like Biltmore Estate, River Arts District, craft breweries.
Attended 'Impressionist Masters' exhibition at City Art Museum today. Loved the Water Lily painting. Particularly drawn to Post-Impressionism section on women artists Mary Cassatt and Berthe Morisot.

Interested in Post-Impressionist women artists: Eva Gonzalès, Frida Kahlo, Sonia Delaunay, Natalia Goncharova. Particularly interested in Eva Gonzalès' 'Une Loge aux Théâtre des Italiens' (1874) depicting sister Jeanne in theater box.

Planning trip to Tokyo in May with flexible dates. Has accumulated 7 air trips in last 3 months. Planning to stay in Roppongi area. Will take bullet train to other parts of Japan and domestic flight from Tokyo to Osaka.

Using United Airlines MileagePlus miles. Needs 70,000 miles for one-way business class Chicago to London during peak season (April-October). Recent flight February 10th Chicago to LA on American Airlines: uncomfortable middle seat, bland chicken sandwich meal, watched 'Joker' and 'Parasite' movies.

Wake up at 6:30 AM, takes approximately 1 hour to get ready: 20-minute meditation session, 30-minute workout, quick breakfast. Looking for high-energy breakfast options and meditation apps like Headspace.
Recently added strength training exercises: upper body Tuesdays and Thursdays, lower body Wednesdays and Fridays. Seeking high-protein lunch ideas to support muscle growth and recovery.

Currently eats oatmeal for breakfast and it's been working well. Interested in trying guava oatmeal with hemp seeds. Exploring adding chia seeds, walnuts, almonds, or hemp seeds for extra nutrition and crunch.

Walmart grocery run last Saturday spent $75: chicken breasts, frozen vegetables, cereal (buy-one-get-one-free deal), 24-pack Diet Coke. Trying to limit snack purchases to every other week. Working on reducing food waste and meal planning.

Recent shopping expenses: school supplies for neighbor's kids $30 (pens, pencils, notebooks, highlighters, lunchbox); mother's day gift being decided; wedding gift for cousin KitchenAid mixer $100 (split cost with sister); roommate IKEA household items $100.

Purchased handmade candle and artisanal soaps from neighborhood holiday market on December 15th. Planning gift wrapping: wrap soaps in kraft paper with twine, add muslin bag with dried lavender buds. Aunt's favorite scents are lavender and vanilla.

Recently tried ukulele at music workshop at local community center. Also tried harmonica and drums, had a blast. Ukulele resonated most. Already taking guitar lessons. Considering mahogany ukulele purchase with $100-150 budget.

Brother just started high school last month, struggling with new schedule and heavy workload. User helping with math homework. Considering Khan Academy, Mathway, IXL for math tutoring support and study routine strategies.

Mother experiencing frequent migraines lately. User trying to support through stress management techniques: deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, meditation, regular exercise, healthy eating, consistent sleep schedule.

Has golden retriever puppy named Max. Started leaving him alone today for short periods. Using interactive toys and puzzle feeders to keep Max engaged: Kong Classic, Tricky Treat Ball, Outward Hound Brick Puzzle, treat-dispensing mats.

Consulted doctor about feeling full quickly after meals. Diagnosed with GERD (Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease). Treating with lifestyle changes: avoiding trigger foods, weight loss, elevating head while sleeping. May use medications: antacids, proton pump inhibitors, H2-receptor blockers.

Just finished binge-watching favorite show. Seeking new sci-fi series on Netflix with complex characters and similar tone. Planning to start with 'Altered Carbon'. Also interested in 'Travelers', 'Lost in Space', 'The OA', 'Colony'.

Started planning home office update today, browsing online for inspiration. Working from home more often. Wants functional comfortable workspace. Considering: ergonomic chair, sit-stand desk, air-purifying plants (snake plant, spider plant, peace lily, ZZ plant, pothos).

Considering sit-stand desk investment due to prolonged sitting causing discomfort. Looking at IKEA Bekant ($300), Varidesk ProPlus ($400), FlexiSpot EC1 ($250). Benefits: improved posture, increased energy, weight loss potential, reduced back pain, increased productivity.

Interested in teaching children financial literacy ages 0-3: age 0 open savings account, age 1 'giving jar', age 2 savings goals, age 3 piggy bank. For ages 20-25: emergency fund, retirement planning, insurance, investment diversification.

Exploring Jesus and Christianity. Interested in kenosis (Christ's self-emptying) doctrine based on Philippians 2:5-8. Studying Catechism teachings about Christ's human nature and Holy Spirit guidance. Understanding Holy Spirit as 'Comforter' or 'Advocate'.
